preview_text:
The model favors Panama to win at 66%, compared to Ghana's 11% chance. This represents a substantial gap between the two sides in the statistical forecast. Ghana enters as a clear underdog in the model's view.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Panama as a heavy favorite, but the draw probability of 24% suggests the match retains meaningful competitive uncertainty. The model does not rate this as a runaway encounter; roughly one in four outcomes could see the teams level after regulation time.

The model and market diverge sharply here. Sportsbooks and prediction markets both assign Ghana near 45% to win and Panama roughly 27%, inverting the model's assessment almost entirely. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these conflicting signals. Traders are pricing Ghana substantially higher than the statistical model does, while viewing Panama's chances as lower than the bias-corrected probabilities suggest.
